description: Teaching comment templates for SGF C[] embedding and YH hint text. V2: two-layer composition (technique + signal).

changelog.author: unknown
changelog.changes: Two-layer composition system (technique phrase + signal phrase) with 15-word cap.

design_principles.precision_over_emission: Never emit a false, vague, or misleading comment. Suppress when uncertain.
design_principles.technique_grounded: Every comment must name the specific technique and its key mechanism.
design_principles.confidence_gated: Comments are only emitted when tag classification confidence is HIGH or CERTAIN.
design_principles.one_insight_rule: Each comment delivers exactly one actionable insight per move.
design_principles.student_learning: Comments exist to teach. Include Japanese/Korean terms where standard.
design_principles.layered_composition: Layer 1 (technique phrase) + Layer 2 (signal phrase) assembled under 15-word cap.

life-and-death.comment: Life & Death (shikatsu) -- find the vital point deciding the group's fate.
life-and-death.vital_move_comment: The vital point -- this decides the group's fate.

living.comment: Living (ikiru) -- create two eyes to ensure your group survives.
living.vital_move_comment: Second eye secured -- the group lives.

ko.comment: Ko (kō) -- start the ko fight that favors your position.

seki.comment: Seki -- achieve mutual life where neither side can attack safely.

capture-race.comment: Capture Race (semeai) -- fill the opponent's liberties before they fill yours.
capture-race.vital_move_comment: Liberty advantage secured -- you win the race.

escape.comment: Escape -- break out of encirclement while keeping your stones connected.
escape.vital_move_comment: The stones break free -- escape succeeds.

snapback.comment: Snapback (uttegaeshi) -- allow the capture, then recapture the larger group.

throw-in.comment: Throw-in (horikomi) -- sacrifice inside the opponent's shape to reduce eye space.
throw-in.vital_move_comment: Eye space destroyed -- the throw-in lands.

ladder.comment: Ladder (shicho) -- each atari forces one response toward the board edge.

net.comment: Net (geta) -- loosely surround stones, preventing escape without direct atari.
net.vital_move_comment: The net closes -- stones cannot escape.

liberty-shortage.comment: Liberty Shortage (damezumari) -- exploit the opponent's lack of liberties to capture.

connect-and-die.comment: Connect & Die (oiotoshi) -- the opponent's connection leads to a larger capture.
connect-and-die.vital_move_comment: Connection forced -- the group is captured.

under-the-stones.comment: Under the Stones (ishi no shita) -- play back into the captured space.
under-the-stones.vital_move_comment: Play beneath -- under the stones completes.

double-atari.comment: Double Atari (ryō-atari) -- threaten two groups at once, capturing at least one.
double-atari.vital_move_comment: Two groups threatened -- one must fall.

vital-point.comment: Vital Point (oki) -- interior placement reduces the opponent's eyes or liberties.
vital-point.vital_move_comment: The vital point -- eyes or liberties collapse.

clamp.comment: Clamp (hasami-tsuke) -- attach inside the opponent's shape to reduce eye space.
clamp.vital_move_comment: The clamp lands -- eye space reduced.

eye-shape.comment: Eye Shape -- create or destroy eyes to decide the group's life or death.
eye-shape.vital_move_comment: Eye shape decided -- the outcome is clear.

dead-shapes.comment: Dead Shapes -- this shape cannot make two eyes regardless of move order.

alias_comments.bent-four: Bent Four (magari-shi-moku) -- dead in the corner without ko.
alias_comments.bulky-five: Bulky Five (guzumi) -- dead; the vital point prevents two eyes.
alias_comments.rabbity-six: Rabbity Six -- dead; no matter who plays first.
alias_comments.l-group: L-Group (eru-gata) -- dead; cannot form two independent eyes.
alias_comments.straight-three: Straight Three (choku-san) -- dead on the first line.
alias_comments.flower-six: Flower Six -- unconditionally dead; no vital point can save it.
alias_comments.table shape: Table Shape -- dead; the rectangular shape cannot form two eyes.
alias_comments.pyramid four: Pyramid Four -- dead; the internal point is the only eye.
alias_comments.crossed five: Crossed Five -- dead; the vital point prevents two eyes.

nakade.comment: Nakade -- play the vital point inside to prevent the opponent making two eyes.
nakade.vital_move_comment: Interior vital point -- two eyes prevented.

connection.comment: Connection (tsugu) -- link separated groups into one to share liberties.

cutting.comment: Cutting (kiri) -- separate the opponent's groups so each must survive alone.
cutting.vital_move_comment: Groups severed -- each must live alone.

alias_comments.crosscut: Crosscut (kiri-chigai) -- cut through the diagonal connection.
alias_comments.peep: Peep (nozoki) -- threaten to cut, forcing a defensive response.
alias_comments.nozoki: Peep (nozoki) -- threaten to cut, forcing a defensive response.

corner.comment: Corner (sumi) -- exploit corner geometry where the board edge limits options.
corner.vital_move_comment: Corner geometry exploited -- edges limit options.

sacrifice.comment: Sacrifice (suteishi) -- give up stones to gain a larger strategic advantage.
sacrifice.vital_move_comment: The sacrifice pays off -- larger gain secured.

shape.comment: Shape (katachi) -- find efficient stone placement that minimizes weaknesses.

endgame.comment: Endgame (yose) -- maximize territorial gain with precise late-game technique.

tesuji.comment: Tesuji -- a sharp tactical move that exploits a positional weakness.

alias_comments.hane: Hane -- bend around the opponent's stone at the key point.
alias_comments.crane's nest: Crane's Nest (tsuru no sugomori) -- trap stones within their own shape.
alias_comments.wedge: Wedge (warikomi) -- insert between the opponent's stones to split them.
alias_comments.tiger's mouth: Tiger's Mouth -- a shape that threatens to capture if entered.
alias_comments.kosumi: Kosumi -- diagonal move creating a flexible, hard-to-cut connection.
alias_comments.keima: Keima -- knight's move for a light, flexible extension.
alias_comments.warikomi: Warikomi -- wedge between stones to disconnect.
alias_comments.nose tesuji: Nose Tesuji (hanatsuke) -- attach at the nose to cut.
alias_comments.descent: Descent (sagari) -- drop down to secure the key point.
alias_comments.sagari: Descent (sagari) -- drop down to secure the key point.
alias_comments.atekomi: Atekomi -- strike directly at the vital point.

joseki.comment: Joseki -- follow the standard corner sequence for an equal result.

fuseki.comment: Fuseki -- choose the optimal opening move for whole-board balance.

assembly_rules.parenthetical_counting: Parenthetical Japanese terms count as 1 word (e.g. '(uttegaeshi)' = 1 word)
assembly_rules.coord_token: {!xy} format (SGF 2-letter coordinate, transform-safe -- never hardcode board positions)

templates.guard: Only when PV depth ≤ 1 and capture verified.

templates.comment: Leads to ko - direct path avoids it.
Close, but not the best move.

voice_constraints.description: VP-1 through VP-5 enforcement rules for teaching comment voice

opponent_response_templates.description: PI-10: Condition-keyed opponent-response phrases appended to wrong-move comments
opponent_response_templates.conditional_dash_rule: Omit dash from opponent-response when wrong-move template already contains em-dash
